product_surfaces: CLI create-issue --live --labels (POST /api/v5/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with labels as native JSON array)
CLI update-issue --live --labels (PATCH /api/v5/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number} with labels as native JSON array)
CLI add-label --live (routed through issue-update or returns unsupported capability diagnostic)
GitCode live adapter CreateIssue/UpdateIssue (DTO Labels field typed json.RawMessage via EncodeIssueLabels)
Service executeWrite/callWriteAdapter (add-label rejected with ErrUnsupportedCapability at service and provider layers)
liveProvider.AddLabel (overridden to return ErrUnsupportedCapability before HTTP call)
mocks_used: httptest.Server (mock GitCode API routes in internal/gitcode/client_test.go for create/update issue endpoints)
liveProvider type assertion with mock server URL (TestScenario013004, TestScenario013006)
json.RawMessage and encoding/json for payload shape assertions
production_files_modified: internal/gitcode/http_client.go (AddLabel implementation present but guarded by provider)
internal/service/service.go (executeWrite rejects add-label at line 1941; callWriteAdapter rejects add-label at line 2098)
internal/service/types.go (WriteCommandRequest retains Labels []string and Label string fields)
internal/cli/cli.go (writeRequest parses --labels into []string; dispatchWrite routes add-label to svc.AddLabel)
internal/gitcode/errors.go (ErrUnsupportedCapability with CapabilityKey and Message fields)

scenarios.description: CLI create-issue --live sends labels as native JSON array in request body; double-encoded string labels are rejected
CLI update-issue --live sends labels as native JSON array in request body; returned labels normalized to cache
CLI add-label --live returns unsupported capability diagnostic at service and provider layers; old add-label endpoint never called
go test ./... and git diff --check pass offline without credentials, network, SSH agent, or Keychain

decommission-1.description: /api/v5/repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}/labels add-label route removed from successful product mutation
decommission-1.verification: Three-layer defense proves the old route is unreachable: (1) service.executeWrite rejects add-label command at line 1941 before any adapter call, (2) service.callWriteAdapter rejects add-label at line 2098, (3) liveProvider.AddLabel returns ErrUnsupportedCapability at provider.go:239 before any HTTP request. Tests confirm: TestScenario013004 uses httptest.Server with /labels path check that fails the test if ever reached; TestScenario013006 uses unreachable server URL to prove no HTTP connection is attempted; TestAddLabelDryRunNoMutation and TestAddLabelLiveUnsupportedCapability assert fake client counters remain 0.

decommission-2.description: GitHub-like labels JSON array request contract for GitCode issue create/update replaced with json.RawMessage encoding
decommission-2.verification: CreateIssueRequest.Labels and UpdateIssueRequest.Labels are typed json.RawMessage (models.go:327,337). EncodeIssueLabels returns json.RawMessage with native JSON array content (label_normalizer.go:9-26). TestScenario013001 and TestScenario013002 capture request bodies and assert labels field is a native JSON array. TestLabel013ArrayLabelsAccepted proves double-encoded string labels are rejected with 400 by the mock server. TestScenario013008 verifies EncodeIssueLabels output is a valid JSON array literal. No []string labels field exists in the issue mutation DTOs.

outcome-3.description: Labels encoded as GitCode-accepted JSON strings, returned label objects normalize into cache source labels, array-payload regressions fail, unsupported old add-label route not silently used
outcome-3.evidence: TestScenario013001 proves labels sent as native JSON array on create. TestScenario013002 proves labels sent as native JSON array on update. TestScenario013004 and TestScenario013006 prove add-label returns ErrUnsupportedCapability and old route is never called. TestScenario013007 proves returned GitCode label objects normalize into cache []string. TestScenario013003 proves no-labels omits the field entirely. TestLabel013ArrayLabelsAccepted proves double-encoded string labels are rejected.

outcome-7.description: Error classification: API validation, schema/decode, credential/configuration, and transport failures are visibly distinct
outcome-7.evidence: TestLabel014SchemaDecodeDistinctFromTransport proves schema_decode is distinct from transport errors. TestLabel015ObjectLabelWithMissingIDReturnsSchemaDecode proves missing label ID produces schema_decode. TestLabel006NormalizeMissingID proves normalization failures produce schema_decode, not transport failures. The add-label ErrUnsupportedCapability is a distinct error type handled separately from the four-class error taxonomy.

outcome-9.description: MCP write tools omitted/read-only; CLI writes remain the supported mutation path
outcome-9.evidence: CLI create-issue and update-issue work with correct label encoding through the live adapter. CLI add-label is explicitly rejected with ErrUnsupportedCapability directing operators to use update-issue --labels. The service and provider layers enforce this at multiple levels. CLI mutation tests (TestQueryCommandsUseServiceOnly) confirm dispatch routing works.

outcome-10.description: go test ./... passes without real credentials, network, SSH agent, or OS Keychain access
outcome-10.evidence: All 14 packages pass offline. All label mutation tests use httptest.Server or fake client. No test requires real credentials, network access, SSH agent, or Keychain. git diff --check passes with no whitespace violations.
